admit.label: Admit
admit.authority: Which proposed requests may enter serving

schedule.label: Schedule
schedule.authority: Which runnable requests receive service and how much

cache.label: Cache
cache.authority: Which resident or prospective objects remain cached

feedback.label: Feedback
feedback.authority: How enacted outcomes update policy state

schedule.summary: Rank work groups by least attained service, with an anti-starvation boost.

feedback.summary: Charge enacted service to request or work-group policy state.

schedule.summary: Prioritize preempted and TTL-pinned work, then apply group-level FCFS.

cache.summary: Keep TTL-active beneficiaries resident and reclaim unpinned state.

feedback.summary: Activate or expire per-request TTL state from enacted outcomes.

schedule.summary: Prioritize requests whose required cache state is already loaded.

cache.summary: Reclaim varying suffixes first and stage prefetch for marked objects.

route.summary: Exploit the longest reusable prefix or explore by load and eviction cost.

schedule.summary: Choose ready critical-path work using cache reuse, cost, and forced progress.
Serve the client with the smallest virtual-token counter.

feedback.summary: Charge observed input and output tokens to each client counter.

route.summary: Minimize new-prefill-tokens times batch-size while excluding hotspots.

admit.summary: Throttle by weighted prior service plus predicted interference cost.

schedule.summary: Order admitted requests by weighted attained service.

feedback.summary: Accumulate enacted service tokens in per-client policy state.

cache.summary: Admit and retain the highest reuse-probability times recompute-FLOPs per byte.

feedback.summary: Maintain enacted cache-feedback accounting for subsequent decisions.

cache.summary: Reclaim a legal leaf with the lowest prefix-aware GDSF value.

route.summary: Trade prefix hits against replica load using the client's deficit credit.

schedule.summary: Order by deficit credit, then by cached-token locality.

feedback.summary: Add fairness quantum and subtract enacted service per client.

schedule.summary: Resume interrupted work first and minimize expected wasted tokens.

cache.summary: Cache when reuse beats recompute, order reclaim, and optionally stage swap.

schedule.summary: Serve the fairness lane first, then the deepest pending demand.

cache.summary: Reclaim by demand depth shared with the scheduler's pending-state view.

admit.summary: Accept requests whose estimated queue wait fits their SLO.

route.summary: Choose the feasible edge with the smallest estimated wait.

schedule.summary: Order work groups by their virtual-queue wait.

feedback.summary: Own the feedback boundary for virtual-queue accounting; the current kernel has no custom reducer.

admit.summary: Accept only requests predicted to complete within their end-to-end SLO.

route.summary: Choose the target with the smallest predicted stage latency.

schedule.summary: Serve the request with the least remaining latency slack first.
Rank by reasoning progress and stage cancellation above the confidence threshold.

feedback.summary: Receive cancellation and progress outcomes; the current kernel has no custom reducer.

schedule.summary: Prioritize work groups with fewer completed branches.

feedback.summary: Increment enacted branch-completion counts in work-group state.

admit.summary: Gate weighted request size against per-queue admission quotas.

schedule.summary: Order by queue class and age requests within each class.

cache.summary: Cache hot adapters, bypass cold ones, and reclaim legal residents.
Admit and prefetch hot prefixes while reclaiming below-threshold residents.

feedback.summary: Accumulate observed prefix reuse counts in shared policy state.

schedule.summary: Stage cancellation for projected deadline misses and schedule the remainder.

feedback.summary: Receive cancellation outcomes; the current kernel has no custom reducer.

admit.summary: Bound admitted branches per work group and by an interference budget.

schedule.summary: Stage cancellation for excess branches and schedule the legal remainder.

route.summary: Use two-choice prefix affinity with SLO risk and optional hotspot rebalance.
Choose minimum virtual usage and optionally stage live rebalancing.

feedback.summary: Record enacted placement and rebalancing feedback volume.

route.summary: Load-balance first turns, preserve follow-up affinity, and migrate tail outliers.

schedule.summary: Run tool-ready work while staging cancellation or migration when needed.

cache.summary: Retain live program state and bypass or reclaim dead state.

feedback.summary: Receive cancellation and rebalance outcomes; the current kernel has no custom reducer.

route.summary: Choose the target with the smallest workflow lookahead cost.

schedule.summary: Order runnable requests by workflow rank.

cache.summary: Use Belady-like next-use reclaim and stage prefetch for marked objects.

feedback.summary: Receive prefetch and enactment outcomes; the current kernel has no custom reducer.

route.summary: Choose just-enough quality at minimum cost and rebalance high-risk work.

feedback.summary: Receive migration outcomes; the current kernel has no custom reducer.

route.summary: Place the heavy first prefill by capacity and preserve follow-up affinity.
Place requests on the target with the smallest dependency distance.

schedule.summary: Allocate service only to requests whose dependencies are ready.

route.summary: Prefer cache locality, then load, with optional work-stealing rebalance.

schedule.summary: Fair-share service by work-group attained service.

cache.summary: Retain live workflow-TTL state and reclaim expired objects.

route.summary: Jointly optimize bounded request-target assignment under target capacity.
